tokens: Man, this is still a slope.
Jack, I'm going to pull around and in the front of the way you're facing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can go down...There's a crater over here, (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to be able to pick up lots of those fragments out in that field out there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, I just came downslope reading 193/3.1; just about 100 meters to pick up Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 48 Yankee has a sample of about a one-third-meter boulder that was lying in...that's sitting right smack dab in a little crater of it's own.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, you just kicked a snowstorm of dust across here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to drop it (the hammer) in the pan here.
Hold on to it, I think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think, (when) we get (to) some more level spots, I can dust this thing (the battery cover) back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Go away from you one twist.
You're...Oh, wait a minute...Get up, get up, get up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, let me get this thing out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got a lot of static now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, you got a rock on your right of your (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay; how about that field...Not this block, but (out where) there's sort of a collection of them...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh; going into the Sun, I can't see a thing to tell you about Wessex Cleft, that we haven't already said.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Station 7 is nominally 208 and 3.3, but it's any group of any significant boulders you want to stop at in reality.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You feel like you're on a downslope over there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I feel like you're about ready to spin out downhill any minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Tongue in cheek) I don't feel that at all up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, it's hard to give you much (description), looking into the Sun the way we are.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We must be about 200 meters up the slope, looking at that little valley (Taurus-Littrow) down there, Jack, on the right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The pattern on the slope really doesn't look much different than on the light mantle.
Matter of fact, it looks very much like light mantle, except for these large blocks that are in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you guys may still have your visors up.
We can't tell, but you might be better off with them down, if you've forgotten that they're up.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: My hands work just as well (as an eye shade) as my visor, as a matter of fact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I can't believe mine could be up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got a crater right in front of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That looks like a pretty good pile to work on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, what heading are you going to want me to park on?
Why don't we get in that flat area, Jack, so I can dust the radiators.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is going to be a very short station.
Probably not more than 10 or 15 minutes.
Just to grab, as I say a maximum variety of hand samples with a minimum amount of documentation and a minimum amount of time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We do a pan, and pick up a lot of those small ones, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We would like to have the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'd like to see us a little more level.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...TV camera and its mirrors and stuff dusted there, however.
But we won't do anything to the battery.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I thought you were going to stop back there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I was going out here around this big one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) See, there's a lot of little ones up in here I want to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do not do anything to the batteries.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't figure out where you're going to stop.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right here to give you as much of a level spot as I can.
That's about as level a spot as I can find.
I'm inside the slope of a crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want me to help you with the dusting, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Only one guy can do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just...You take a pan "before", and we'll start picking up some of those samples, and I'll take a pan afterward.
(Pause) See what kind of variety we can get here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There is another one of our blue-gray breccias, I think, over there: re-crystallized breccias with some of that crushed anorthosite in it.
I'm going to take the pan at about...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, what's your frame count?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I'm going to take the pan at 11 feet (focus), so you can see the fragments that we're going to pick up here.
Then we can take another one for location work.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, I repeat, we'd like to get some dusting of the (TCU) mirror and the lens of the TV; (dust the) TCU and the TV.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me get you out of the Sun.
(Pause) I wouldn't do this for anybody but you, you know that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You notice the temperature difference with that high Sun angle?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're probably letting in a lot of infrared through without having that gold visor down, too.
That's sort of an infrared shield.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, but mine's been down all the time, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 540 is the first bag of selected samples.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to leave you (Gene's SCB) open, but don't let me...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get...Here, put that one in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wait a minute, let's get a bag on it.
We're getting too many rocks, and we don't know where they came from.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think it will fit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we'll wrap it a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, wait a minute, it will fit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 541 is partially around another big rock in Gene's collection bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you get pictures of this thing here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah; well, not the big rock yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I was just collecting (fragments) in this area.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you keep grabbing a few, and I'm going to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's one of the blue-gray rocks, Bob.
And it's got a light-colored fragment that runs the full height of it, about a meter and a half thick.
And then it's got the blue-gray rock on the other side.
As a matter of fact...(Pause) Let me look at it closely.
(Pause) It's a fragment in it all right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And remember to document around the corner if you're trying to get some photo-documentation of the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I wouldn't be absolutely positive, but it sure looks like I see a dikelet in here, that's in the inclusion.
And I'm going to get a close-up stereo of it.
I'd call it a dikelet, if you pinned me down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wish I could break a sample right off.
There's three or four of them!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) The material in the dike looks...Yeah, it is, it is, it (the blue-gray, dike material)'s not covering it (the light colored host rock).
It's between the lighter-colored rock, and it's the blue-gray rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, let me get rid of this.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got...I got...Well, maybe it isn't a dikelet.
Maybe it's just a screen covering, a flow covering.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Putting the soil sample in Gene's SCB) They're little veinlets of...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get this whole thing in a bag.
It's fractured, primarily around the dike.
It's in several pieces, but we're going to put it all in one bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, man, they're going to have to assemble that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here, I got...I got...Let me get it piece by piece.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need to get a...Put one of those dikes in another bag.
(Pause) Bob, it looks like some fraction of the blue-gray material has obviously...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, can you get that dike there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Getting his hammer) I can get it right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I think...No, get the piece with the...You get more of it, right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Hitting the boulder eight times, swinging the hammer through relatively large arcs on the later strokes) Yeah.
(Pause) It's this soft, white inclusion again.
(Pause) Oh, it's got to be a dike.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Putting the sample bag in Gene's SCB) Okay, 544.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, yeah, it is, because I just broke into it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we'd like to have you guys moving again in 5 minutes to get to Station 8 on time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Under Bob) Well, you don't have...(Stops to listen to Bob) Yes, sir.
(Pause) Looks like, although the blue-gray up on the hill looked like a fragment breccia, if this is still related, then it's been (through) some partial melting at some time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a preserved contact between the dike and the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't we get this big piece of dike now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There, you've still got some contact there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, these dikes are a dark bluish-gray.
And it looks they're very finely crystalline - maybe with some...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to get...Well...We ought to get a piece of the normal gray that the dikes are coming from.
(Pause) You got your hammer handy still?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause; handing Jack the hammer) I want to get this...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, over here on this side, it looks like the vesicular anorthositic gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to get some regular pictures around this side.
Here's the...(Pause) That one won't come off; this one will.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a sample of the gray - looks like re-crystallized breccia - that the dikes are continuous with.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And a...Bob, it's my turn to say "and a".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me finish the stereo around the corner here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you guys have dropped the scoop there on the ground.
And we're ready for you guys to leave.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And you might grab one FSR (Football-Sized Rock) on the way out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The vesicular anorthositic gabbro is in...What is it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get this in there, and you take the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to get the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Then (I'll) make sure your bag (SCB) is closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yes, I've got to check yours, too.
Let me get uphill from you, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This is one of the worst bags (SCB) we've had.
The latching, it just doesn't stay down.
(Pause) If we get time somewhere, we ought to change that out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: One side of your bag is undone again.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, I'll get it for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Here's a football-size rock that was 50 percent buried.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Remind me to get your bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you see the way I handled that hammer!?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Tell you what, I'm getting accustomed to things.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That gate's a little sluggish, too, Jack.
(Pause) Boy, I think everything is so full of dust (that) nothing wants to move any more.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That one (the FSR) looked like a piece of the gray rock, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Mounting something on the gate) You know, I'll bet I didn't push the gravimeter here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, you're going to have to close the gate, and I'll have to hold the big bag over the top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, before you leave, we'd like you to change mags before you leave this station.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Still struggling with the sticky gate latch) One of those little...Okay, now you can push.
Wait a minute, wait a minute, wait a minute.
(Let me) see what's going on in there.
This thing isn't released all the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, the bag's in the way.
Let me get the (big) bag out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, you might get the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Wait a minute, wait a minute, wait a minute.
(Pause) Let me lift it up and (then you) do it.
(Pause) Well, wait a minute, I've got to tweak this thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Why don't you play with it, and I'll see if I can change a (film) mag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just push that latch...That latch is just sticking, that's all.
I don't know what you do about those problems.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, what magazine did you want, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, you might spend your time taking a...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...standard 74-foot pan while Jack is changing his mag, if you got a chance there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And that's exactly what I'll do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't mind going uphill, because it's so much fun coming down.
Down in my little hole here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, Challenger looks a long way away.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Turning to look at Gene) Bob, those two bags with the goodies in them, will have enough soil to be representative of the area we sampled, too, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And did you guys get your bags fixed up there, Jack?
We were concerned about your SCB for a while.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Look at my camera lens and see how dirty it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Working on Jack's SCB) Now, it's the other hook that came...Turn a little more left.
(Pause) No, it didn't come off, I don't think, unless it...The bottom's off, but the bottom is not going to stay on.
And it's not...You're not going to lose it.
The tops are so tight you'll...Your bottom's loose, but that's because your harness shrunk a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looked like, from time to time, guys, that only one of Jack's hooks was hooked.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: They're both on, and they're both tight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...I got the bottom hooked again, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But the bottom is not going to stay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and, 17, as you get around to the front there, Gene, would you dust the LCRU; we don't think you did that here, and the top of the TV camera.
And, Jack, would you take a peek at the SEP temperature for us?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I already did that when we stopped at the station.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, this is tied down everywhere.
You're just going to have to watch it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Hey, we seem to do an awful lot of down-Sun driving for - (correcting himself) or up-Sun driving - for all the planning we did.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait until we come home from station 8, then we'll take care of it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Gene, as you get started there, we'd like a couple of Rover battery temperatures; at least one, we know what the other one says.
And, Jack, we might get a frame count from you...Oh, excuse me, we already got that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, okay, (the battery temperature is) 110; and CDR, by the way, is about 73 on the frames.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
